description_long: This is a small, weathered barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in overlapping wooden shingles. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, showing signs of age and wear, with some areas featuring visible cracks and peeling paint. One side of the barn has a single, narrow door framed by vertical trim, while another side displays a small, rectangular hatch or plaque. The gable ends are accentuated with horizontal boards painted in a contrasting color, and one side is partially overgrown with vegetation. The barn sits on a slightly raised base, suggesting it is meant to be placed directly on the ground in an outdoor setting. There are no visible windows, and the structure appears compact and utilitarian, typical of outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban environments.
description: A small, aged barn with a steep gable roof, horizontal wood siding, and a single narrow door. The structure is weathered, with peeling paint and some overgrown vegetation on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the long side of the barn with weathered horizontal wood siding and the full expanse of the shingled gable roof.
description_view_1: This end view highlights the gable with horizontal boards, a small rectangular hatch or plaque, and no visible door.
description_view_2: This side features the main narrow door, framed by vertical trim, and the shingled roof above.
description_view_3: This gable end is partially covered with green vegetation and has no visible openings or hatches.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with wooden shingles
